page: Microsoft Azure B20ms is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The configuration provides 20 vCPUs and 20 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is 80.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 164 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is consistently weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations. Conversely, multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only small block cached and large block uncached operations, also register in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the burstable CPU allocation model allows for resource density advantages, making it suitable for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all perform within the average tier. However, the instance exhibits poor to weak performance for LLM text generation, making it less suitable for continuous generative AI tasks. Recommended workloads include general-purpose applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, where burstable CPU credits can be accumulated during idle periods and utilized during peak demand.
description: Microsoft Azure B20ms is a burstable virtual machine instance from the BS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 20 vCPUs, 20 physical cores, 80.0 GB of RAM, and 164 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance lacks GPU accelerators and public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark results reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, falling in the bottom 25%, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations reside in the average tier. Qualitatively, the burstable CPU allocation model offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads with fluctuating demand. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, rather than continuous high-compute tasks like LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B20ms is a burstable virtual machine featuring 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 80.0 GB of RAM, and 164 GB of local storage, offering average multi-core performance for variable workloads.

tagline: A burstable virtual machine featuring twenty cores and eighty gigabytes of memory, balancing variable compute needs with local storage capabilities.
